Caption: Screening of carbon allotropes for their application to LIBs based on theoretical and electrochemical investigations and their promising recyclability.

(A) Structural comparison of graphite at low-rate (0.05C) and high-rate (0.5C) lithiation to the same capacity (250 mAh/g). (i) The voltage curves, XRD patterns, and (ii, iv) the iFFT images of the graphite discharged at 0.05C (black) and 0.5C [25]. (B) Schematic of 3 × 3 × 1 supercell configuration of graphite and possible Li adsorption sites. (i) top view (ii)–(iv) binding energy and diffusion barrier for possible pathways. GRa: Li diffuses from a hollow site (Hb) in the bottom layer through a bridge site (Bb) to an adjacent hollow site (Hb). GRb: Li diffuses from a hollow site (Hb) in the bottom layer through the middle (Mc) of a C atom to a hollow site (Ht) in the top layer. GRc: Li diffuses from a hollow site (Hb) in the bottom layer through the middle (Ml) to Ht in the top layer, in a zigzag path [27]. Panel A: Reprinted with permission from Ref.
